chief_complaint: A 65-year-old male with a 10-year history of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) presents with shortness of breath and fatigue.
history: The patient has been experiencing progressive shortness of breath over the past year, with a recent exacerbation requiring hospitalization. He has a 30-pack-year smoking history and has been on bronchodilators and inhaled corticosteroids. He has been prescribed long-term oxygen therapy (LTOT) for 15 hours a day, but his oxygen saturation remains low.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 90 bpm, RR 24/min, SpO2 88% on room air. Physical examination reveals bilateral wheezing and crackles.
labs: Arterial blood gas: pH 7.35, PaO2 55 mmHg, PaCO2 45 mmHg. ECG: normal. Chest X-ray: hyperinflation of the lungs.
